Operation 'post-transferFunds' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description explains the operation's purpose and constraints well, but lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. Added: (1) explicit mention of expected request schema contents (source account, destination account, amount, transfer code), (2) response schema details (transfer status and reference information), and (3) side effects (immediate balance modification). These additions help AI agents understand what data to provide and what to expect in return, improving interpretability for fund transfer scenarios.

data.current_description: Transfers funds from one account to another account. Both accounts must be in the same platform, but can have different account holders. The transfer must include a transfer code, which should be determined by the platform, in compliance with local regulations.
data.description_suggestion: Transfers funds from one account to another account within the same platform. Both source and destination accounts can have different account holders. The transfer requires a transfer code determined by the platform in compliance with local regulations. Expects a TransferFundsRequest containing source account, destination account, amount, and transfer code. Returns a TransferFundsResponse with transfer status and reference information. The operation modifies account balances immediately upon successful execution.
data.reasoning: The current description explains the operation's purpose and constraints well, but lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. Added: (1) explicit mention of expected request schema contents (source account, destination account, amount, transfer code), (2) response schema details (transfer status and reference information), and (3) side effects (immediate balance modification). These additions help AI agents understand what data to provide and what to expect in return, improving interpretability for fund transfer scenarios.

message: Operation 'post-accountHolderTransactionList' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about pagination requirements and input parameters needed for filtering. Enhanced summary emphasizes pagination aspect. Improved description clarifies input requirements (filters), pagination mechanism, and sorting behavior for better AI agent understanding of how to use this operation iteratively.

data.current_description: Returns a list of transactions for an account holder's accounts. You can specify the accounts and transaction statuses to be included on the list. The call returns a maximum of 50 transactions for each account. To retrieve all transactions, you must make another call with the 'page' value incremented. Transactions are listed in chronological order, with the most recent transaction first.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve paginated transaction history for account holder
data.description_suggestion: Returns a paginated list of transactions for an account holder's accounts. Supports filtering by specific accounts and transaction statuses. Each response returns a maximum of 50 transactions per account; use the 'page' parameter to retrieve additional transactions. Transactions are sorted chronologically with the most recent first. Requires AccountHolderTransactionListRequest with account and status filters.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about pagination requirements and input parameters needed for filtering. Enhanced summary emphasizes pagination aspect. Improved description clarifies input requirements (filters), pagination mechanism, and sorting behavior for better AI agent understanding of how to use this operation iteratively.

message: Operation 'post-debitAccountHolder' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_side_effects. Original summary is vague about the direction of funds flow. Description mentions webhook notification but doesn't clearly explain the asynchronous nature or that this is a top-up operation. Enhanced summary clarifies the action and direction. Improved description emphasizes asynchronous processing, webhook notification mechanism, and split instruction handling for AI agents to understand the operation's full lifecycle and side effects.

data.current_description:
Sends a direct debit request to an account holder's bank account. If the direct debit is successful, the funds are settled in the accounts specified in the split instructions. Adyen sends the result of the direct debit in a [`DIRECT_DEBIT_INITIATED`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/NotificationService/latest/post/DIRECT_DEBIT_INITIATED) notification webhook.

 To learn more about direct debits, see [Top up accounts](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/top-up-accounts).
data.summary_suggestion: Initiate direct debit to account holder's bank account
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a direct debit request from an account holder's bank account to fund platform accounts. Upon successful processing, funds are distributed to specified accounts according to split instructions. The operation is asynchronous; Adyen sends the result via a DIRECT_DEBIT_INITIATED webhook notification. Funds settlement follows the split configuration provided in the request.
data.reasoning: Original summary is vague about the direction of funds flow. Description mentions webhook notification but doesn't clearly explain the asynchronous nature or that this is a top-up operation. Enhanced summary clarifies the action and direction. Improved description emphasizes asynchronous processing, webhook notification mechanism, and split instruction handling for AI agents to understand the operation's full lifecycle and side effects.

message: Operation 'post-payoutAccountHolder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate and action-oriented. Description is too minimal and lacks critical details about what inputs are required, what the operation returns, and the settlement process. Enhanced description clarifies input requirements (account, amount, currency), the external settlement nature, and expected response format for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_summary: Pay out from an account to the account holder
data.current_description: Pays out a specified amount from an account to the bank account of account holder.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a payout of a specified amount from a platform account to the account holder's designated bank account. The operation transfers funds out of the platform and settles them to the external bank account. Requires PayoutAccountHolderRequest with account identifier, amount, and currency. Returns payout confirmation with transaction reference and status.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate and action-oriented. Description is too minimal and lacks critical details about what inputs are required, what the operation returns, and the settlement process. Enhanced description clarifies input requirements (account, amount, currency), the external settlement nature, and expected response format for better AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'post-refundFundsTransfer' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks specificity about what inputs are needed to identify the transfer to refund and what the operation returns. Enhanced description clarifies that a transfer reference is required, explains the constraint about same-platform accounts, and specifies the response includes confirmation and updated balances for AI agent clarity.

data.current_description: Refunds funds transferred from one account to another. Both accounts must be in the same platform, but can have different account holders.
data.description_suggestion: Reverses a previously executed funds transfer between two platform accounts. Both source and destination accounts must belong to the same platform but can have different account holders. Requires the original transfer reference or transaction identifier. Returns refund confirmation with new transaction reference and updated account balances.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks specificity about what inputs are needed to identify the transfer to refund and what the operation returns. Enhanced description clarifies that a transfer reference is required, explains the constraint about same-platform accounts, and specifies the response includes confirmation and updated balances for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'post-refundNotPaidOutTransfers' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear but could be more concise. Description explains the split payment logic well but lacks clarity on required inputs and expected outputs. Enhanced description clarifies the account-specific nature of the operation, maintains the split payment explanation, and adds specifics about required input (account identifier) and response format (refund summary with totals) for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Refund all transactions of an account since the most recent payout
data.current_description: Refunds all the transactions of an account that have taken place since the most recent payout. This request is on a account basis (as opposed to a payment basis), so only the portion of the payment that was made to the specified account is refunded. The commissions, fees, and payments to other accounts remain in the accounts to which they were sent as designated by the original payment's split details.
data.description_suggestion: Refunds all transactions credited to a specified account since its most recent payout, on an account-by-account basis. When a transaction involved split payments, only the portion allocated to the specified account is refunded; commissions, fees, and amounts sent to other accounts per the original split instructions remain unchanged. Requires account identifier. Returns refund summary with total amount refunded and affected transaction count.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but could be more concise. Description explains the split payment logic well but lacks clarity on required inputs and expected outputs. Enhanced description clarifies the account-specific nature of the operation, maintains the split payment explanation, and adds specifics about required input (account identifier) and response format (refund summary with totals) for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'post-setupBeneficiary' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear but verbose. Description explains the relationship and automatic redirection well but lacks specifics about constraints validation, required inputs, and response format. Enhanced summary is more concise. Improved description clarifies the immediate balance transfer, automatic redirection mechanism, chain limitations, required inputs (account identifiers), and response format (confirmation with balances) for comprehensive AI agent understanding of this complex operation.

data.current_summary: Designate a beneficiary account and transfer the benefactor's current balance
data.current_description: Defines a benefactor and a beneficiary relationship between two accounts. At the time of benefactor/beneficiary setup, the funds in the benefactor account are transferred to the beneficiary account, and any further payments to the benefactor account are automatically sent to the beneficiary account. A series of benefactor/beneficiaries may not exceed four beneficiaries and may not have a cycle in it.
data.summary_suggestion: Establish beneficiary relationship and transfer benefactor balance
data.description_suggestion: Establishes a benefactor-to-beneficiary relationship between two accounts, automatically redirecting all future payments. Upon setup, the current balance in the benefactor account is immediately transferred to the beneficiary account. Subsequently, any payments received by the benefactor account are automatically forwarded to the beneficiary account. Beneficiary chains are limited to a maximum of four levels and cannot contain cycles. Requires both account identifiers. Returns confirmation with transfer status and new account balances.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but verbose. Description explains the relationship and automatic redirection well but lacks specifics about constraints validation, required inputs, and response format. Enhanced summary is more concise. Improved description clarifies the immediate balance transfer, automatic redirection mechanism, chain limitations, required inputs (account identifiers), and response format (confirmation with balances) for comprehensive AI agent understanding of this complex operation.
